model.adv.stg_a.name: stg_a
model.adv.stg_a.raw_code:
-- base table A
select
    id,
    user_id,
    amount,
    status,
    created_at
from {{ source('raw', 'table_a') }}
model.adv.stg_a.compiled_code:
-- base table A
select
    id,
    user_id,
    amount,
    status,
    created_at
from table_a

model.adv.stg_b.name: stg_b
model.adv.stg_b.raw_code:
-- base table B; deliberately shares column names with stg_a (amount, status)
select
    id,
    user_id,
    amount,
    status,
    region
from {{ source('raw', 'table_b') }}

model.adv.adv_01_select_star.name: adv_01_select_star
model.adv.adv_01_select_star.raw_code:
-- All output columns come from stg_a. Parser must expand * to stg_a's column set.
select *
from {{ ref('stg_a') }}
where status = 'active'
model.adv.adv_01_select_star.compiled_code:
-- All output columns come from stg_a. Parser must expand * to stg_a's column set.
select *
from stg_a
where status = 'active'

model.adv.adv_02_qualified_star.name: adv_02_qualified_star
model.adv.adv_02_qualified_star.raw_code:
-- a.* expands to stg_a columns only; email is the lone explicit add from stg_users.
select
    a.*,
    u.email
from {{ ref('stg_a') }} a
left join {{ ref('stg_users') }} u on a.user_id = u.user_id
model.adv.adv_02_qualified_star.compiled_code:
-- a.* expands to stg_a columns only; email is the lone explicit add from stg_users.
select
    a.*,
    u.email
from stg_a a
left join stg_users u on a.user_id = u.user_id

model.adv.adv_03_union.name: adv_03_union
model.adv.adv_03_union.raw_code:
-- output col "amount" derives from stg_a.amount AND stg_b.amount (positional union).
-- output col "source_label" is a literal (no lineage).
select
    id,
    amount,
    'a' as source_label
from {{ ref('stg_a') }}
union all
select
    id,
    amount,
    'b' as source_label
from {{ ref('stg_b') }}
model.adv.adv_03_union.compiled_code:
-- output col "amount" derives from stg_a.amount AND stg_b.amount (positional union).
-- output col "source_label" is a literal (no lineage).
select
    id,
    amount,
    'a' as source_label
from stg_a
union all
select
    id,
    amount,
    'b' as source_label
from stg_b

model.adv.adv_04_ambiguous_column.name: adv_04_ambiguous_column
model.adv.adv_04_ambiguous_column.raw_code:
-- "amount" and "status" are unqualified but exist in BOTH stg_a and stg_b.
-- "user_id" is the join key, present in both. Resolver must decide/flag.
-- created_at exists only in stg_a so it is unambiguous.
select
    user_id,
    amount,
    status,
    created_at
from {{ ref('stg_a') }} a
join {{ ref('stg_b') }} b on a.id = b.id
model.adv.adv_04_ambiguous_column.compiled_code:
-- "amount" and "status" are unqualified but exist in BOTH stg_a and stg_b.
-- "user_id" is the join key, present in both. Resolver must decide/flag.
-- created_at exists only in stg_a so it is unambiguous.
select
    user_id,
    amount,
    status,
    created_at
from stg_a a
join stg_b b on a.id = b.id

model.adv.adv_05_self_join.name: adv_05_self_join
model.adv.adv_05_self_join.raw_code:
-- child.user_id and parent_user_id both trace to stg_a.user_id (different rows, same lineage).
select
    child.id          as id,
    child.user_id     as user_id,
    parent.user_id    as parent_user_id,
    parent.amount     as parent_amount
from {{ ref('stg_a') }} child
left join {{ ref('stg_a') }} parent on child.user_id = parent.id
model.adv.adv_05_self_join.compiled_code:
-- child.user_id and parent_user_id both trace to stg_a.user_id (different rows, same lineage).
select
    child.id          as id,
    child.user_id     as user_id,
    parent.user_id    as parent_user_id,
    parent.amount     as parent_amount
from stg_a child
left join stg_a parent on child.user_id = parent.id

model.adv.adv_06_cte_shadow.name: adv_06_cte_shadow
model.adv.adv_06_cte_shadow.raw_code:
-- The CTE is literally named stg_b. Inside the final select, "stg_b" must resolve to the
-- CTE (which reads stg_a), NOT the real stg_b model. A naive resolver wrongly links stg_b.
with stg_b as (
    select id, user_id, amount
    from {{ ref('stg_a') }}
)
select
    id,
    user_id,
    amount
from stg_b
model.adv.adv_06_cte_shadow.compiled_code:
-- The CTE is literally named stg_b. Inside the final select, "stg_b" must resolve to the
-- CTE (which reads stg_a), NOT the real stg_b model. A naive resolver wrongly links stg_b.
with stg_b as (
    select id, user_id, amount
    from stg_a
)
select
    id,
    user_id,
    amount
from stg_b

model.adv.adv_07_cte_reuse.name: adv_07_cte_reuse
model.adv.adv_07_cte_reuse.raw_code:
with base as (
    select user_id, amount from {{ ref('stg_a') }}
),
agg as (
    select user_id, sum(amount) as total_amount from base group by user_id
)
select
    b.user_id,
    b.amount,
    a.total_amount
from base b            -- base used a second time here
join agg a on b.user_id = a.user_id
model.adv.adv_07_cte_reuse.compiled_code:
with base as (
    select user_id, amount from stg_a
),
agg as (
    select user_id, sum(amount) as total_amount from base group by user_id
)
select
    b.user_id,
    b.amount,
    a.total_amount
from base b            -- base used a second time here
join agg a on b.user_id = a.user_id

model.adv.adv_08_scalar_subquery.name: adv_08_scalar_subquery
model.adv.adv_08_scalar_subquery.raw_code:
-- max_user_amount derives from stg_a.amount via a correlated scalar subquery.
select
    u.user_id,
    u.email,
    (select max(a.amount)
       from {{ ref('stg_a') }} a
      where a.user_id = u.user_id) as max_user_amount
from {{ ref('stg_users') }} u
model.adv.adv_08_scalar_subquery.compiled_code:
-- max_user_amount derives from stg_a.amount via a correlated scalar subquery.
select
    u.user_id,
    u.email,
    (select max(a.amount)
       from stg_a a
      where a.user_id = u.user_id) as max_user_amount
from stg_users u

model.adv.adv_09_derived_table.name: adv_09_derived_table
model.adv.adv_09_derived_table.compiled_code:
select
    sub.user_id,
    sub.amt_doubled
from (
    select user_id, amount * 2 as amt_doubled
    from stg_a
) sub
where sub.amt_doubled > 100

model.adv.adv_10_fanout_expr.name: adv_10_fanout_expr
model.adv.adv_10_fanout_expr.raw_code:
-- amount feeds BOTH net and tax_portion (one input -> two outputs).
-- combined feeds from amount AND status (two inputs -> one output via case).
select
    user_id,
    amount - 5            as net,
    amount * 0.1          as tax_portion,
    case when status = 'active' then amount else 0 end as combined
from {{ ref('stg_a') }}
model.adv.adv_10_fanout_expr.compiled_code:
-- amount feeds BOTH net and tax_portion (one input -> two outputs).
-- combined feeds from amount AND status (two inputs -> one output via case).
select
    user_id,
    amount - 5            as net,
    amount * 0.1          as tax_portion,
    case when status = 'active' then amount else 0 end as combined
from stg_a

model.adv.adv_11_window.name: adv_11_window
model.adv.adv_11_window.raw_code:
-- row_num derives from user_id (partition) + created_at (order).
-- running_total derives from amount + user_id + created_at.
select
    id,
    user_id,
    row_number() over (partition by user_id order by created_at) as row_num,
    sum(amount) over (partition by user_id order by created_at) as running_total
from {{ ref('stg_a') }}
model.adv.adv_11_window.compiled_code:
-- row_num derives from user_id (partition) + created_at (order).
-- running_total derives from amount + user_id + created_at.
select
    id,
    user_id,
    row_number() over (partition by user_id order by created_at) as row_num,
    sum(amount) over (partition by user_id order by created_at) as running_total
from stg_a

model.adv.adv_12_quoted_idents.name: adv_12_quoted_idents
model.adv.adv_12_quoted_idents.raw_code:
-- Output uses quoted mixed-case aliases; "User Id" maps to stg_a.user_id.
select
    user_id    as "User Id",
    amount     as "Amount USD",
    status     as "Status"
from {{ ref('stg_a') }}

model.adv.adv_13_rename_chain.name: adv_13_rename_chain
model.adv.adv_13_rename_chain.raw_code:
-- amount -> gross (here). A downstream model then reads gross. Tests chained renames.
select
    user_id,
    amount as gross,
    status as state
from {{ ref('stg_a') }}
model.adv.adv_13_rename_chain.compiled_code:

    user_id,
    amount as gross,
    status as state
from stg_a

model.adv.adv_14_rename_chain_downstream.name: adv_14_rename_chain_downstream
model.adv.adv_14_rename_chain_downstream.raw_code:
-- net_gross traces back through adv_13.gross -> stg_a.amount.
select
    user_id,
    gross as net_gross,
    state
from {{ ref('adv_13_rename_chain') }}
where state = 'active'
model.adv.adv_14_rename_chain_downstream.compiled_code:
-- net_gross traces back through adv_13.gross -> stg_a.amount.

    user_id,
    gross as net_gross,
    state
from adv_13_rename_chain
where state = 'active'

model.adv.adv_15_dead_column.name: adv_15_dead_column
model.adv.adv_15_dead_column.raw_code:
-- region is pulled into the CTE but never selected downstream.
-- A correct column-lineage graph should show region as NOT contributing to any output.
with enriched as (
    select id, user_id, amount, region
    from {{ ref('stg_b') }}
)
select
    id,
    user_id,
    amount
from enriched
